亚马逊和沃尔玛正在增加他们在美国3600亿美元的处方药市场中抓住市场份额的努力。
 如今，亚马逊宣布，总成员可以获得六个月的几种广泛规定的药物供应，从6美元开始。许多毒品都是奖金，但仍然相对于典型现金价格折扣。昨天沃尔玛表示，通过其沃尔玛+ RX服务购买的处方药将增加高达85％的新折扣，平均储蓄约为65％。
 零售庞然大物在很大程度上未能破坏传统的药房行业，新公告即将来临。大名称仍然是占主导地位的，CVS和Walgreens按收入排名。沃尔玛为其药店具有重要的物理足迹，第五。
 亚马逊尚未制作一个凹陷，甚至不开裂前15名，而且电子商务零售商的野心进一步推移，零售药房最近相对于邮件药房繁殖。虽然该公司去年发布了亚马逊药房的宣布 - 一些分析师预测，CVS和Walgreens将在2018年购买Pillpack 7.53亿美元购买Pillpack之后，该公司已在处方药业务中受到严重打击。亚马逊在市场上缺乏牵引是让电子商务公司拆除它遭遇的每种遗产模型的叙述。
 这可能是为什么上个月从商业人士签到的报告表示，亚马逊正在观察其超过350个全食物的传统药店。但到目前为止，没有任何东西已经过来，因为开始物理药房昂贵。除了商店布局的变化之外，每个新的整个食品都需要大约三名药剂师和五名技术人员。
    亚马逊也很难获得较低价格的毒品。大多数批发购买都是通过制药福利管理人员（PBMS）制作的，该管理人员（PBMS）是中间人，采用采购能力来谈判药品制造商的折扣和降低价格。然后，PBMS将这些扣资的一部分传递给保险公司，尽管小公司和保险公司已声称他们没有看到同样的储蓄，这很难衡量，因为折扣经常秘密。
 当然，药店卖给消费者，但结算与客户是否有保险。如果是这样，药房会收取一个预先谈判的率，并通过PBM重新偿还另一种较低的速度。如果不是，药店将客户充电预定的“通常和习惯”的现金率。这些利率差异很大，因为PBMS将不会报销其上方的药房，药房一直在设定相对较高的通常和常规率。这允许像亚马逊，沃尔玛和Goodrx这样的公司声明眼睛弹出折扣。 
来自亚马逊和沃尔玛的新计划，允许客户完全索引，但不是PBMS。沃尔玛的作品非常像典型的处方药计划。客户申请了一个看起来像沃尔玛发布的处方药卡，只能在其商店或其网站上使用的处方药卡。零售商正在与PBM合作，它似乎通过了一部分典型折扣，以否则将支付较高和习惯性的现金顾客。
 亚马逊正在做同样的事情，但它试图通过向批量送售顾客的药物，减少运输和处理成本来删除更多节省。并非所有药物都可以在六个月的用品中提供，其中一些价格明显超过每月6美元。但有些人包括糖尿病药物二甲双胍，每月1美元可用。如果最终会使购买更便宜，客户仍然可以选择使用他们的保险。
 这是亚马逊的第二次或第三次破坏医疗市场的尝试，具体取决于你的统计方式。它的PillPack购买进入公司的机器人增强型图像 - 启动自身通过使用机器人将所有客户的处方药包装成小型数据包。多年前亚马逊还试图达到健康保险业行业的成立避风港以及伯克希尔赫舍省和摩根大学等公司。这项努力在2月份的失败结束。
note_en:
Amazon and Walmart are ramping up their efforts to grab market share in the $360 billion prescription drug market in the US.
 Today, Amazon  announced that Prime members can receive a six-month supply of several widely prescribed drugs, starting at $6. Many drugs are pricier but are still discounted relative to typical cash prices. And  yesterday, Walmart said it would be adding new discounts of up to 85 percent on prescription drugs purchased through its Walmart+ RX service, with an average savings of about 65 percent.
 The new announcements come as the retail behemoths have largely failed to disrupt the traditional pharmacy industry. Big names still dominate, with CVS and Walgreens topping the rankings by revenue. Walmart, which has a significant physical footprint for its pharmacies, comes in fifth.
 Amazon has yet to make a dent, not even cracking the  top 15, and the e-commerce retailer’s ambitions have been further stymied by the fact that retail pharmacy locations have  thrived recently relative to mail pharmacies. Though the company made waves last year with its announcement of Amazon Pharmacy—some analysts predicted that CVS and Walgreens would be hit hardest by the reveal—the company has been in the prescription drug business for years following its  2018 purchase of PillPack for $753 million. Amazon’s lack of traction in the market is upsetting the narrative that the e-commerce company dismantles every legacy business model it encounters.
 That could be why a report last month from  Business Insider said that Amazon was looking at opening traditional pharmacies inside its more than 350 Whole Foods locations. But nothing has come of the idea so far, as starting a physical pharmacy is expensive. Each new Whole Foods location would need about three pharmacists and five technicians, in addition to changes in store layouts.
    It’s also hard for Amazon to get drugs for lower prices. Most wholesale purchases are made through pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs), which act as middlemen, using purchasing power to negotiate rebates and lower prices from drug manufacturers. PBMs then pass on a portion of those rebates to insurers, though smaller companies and insurers have  claimed that they’re not seeing the same savings, which are hard to measure since the rebates are often secret.
 Pharmacies then sell to consumers, of course, but the billing differs depending on whether the customer has insurance. If so, the pharmacy charges one pre-negotiated rate and is reimbursed by the PBM at another, lower rate. If not, pharmacies charge the customer a predetermined “usual and customary” cash rate. These rates vary widely, and because PBMs won’t reimburse pharmacies above them, pharmacies have been setting relatively high usual and customary rates. That allows companies like Amazon, Walmart, and GoodRx  to claim eye-popping discounts.
 Both new plans from Amazon and Walmart allow customers to sidestep insurance altogether, but not PBMs. Walmart’s works much like a typical prescription drug plan. Customers apply for a “savings card” that looks like a prescription drug card issued by Walmart, usable only at its stores or its website. The retailer is working with a PBM, and it appears to be passing on a significant portion of the typical rebates to cash customers who would otherwise pay the higher usual and customary rate.
 Amazon is doing the same thing, but it's attempting to wring out more savings by sending customers drugs in bulk, reducing shipping and handling costs. Not all drugs are available in six-month supplies, and some that are cost significantly more than $6 per month. But some, including the diabetes medication metformin, are available for $1 per month. Customers can still opt to use their insurance if it ends up making the purchase cheaper.
 This is Amazon’s second or third attempt to disrupt health care markets, depending on how you count. Its PillPack purchase fed into the company’s robot-enhanced image—the startup distinguished itself by using robots to package all of a customer’s prescription drugs into small daily packets. Amazon also tried to upend the health insurance industry years ago when it founded Haven along with companies like Berkshire Hathaway and JPMorgan. That effort ended  in failure this February.
title: 亚马逊和沃尔玛试图升级处方药价格
title_en: Amazon and Walmart try—again—to upend prescription drug prices

note_en:
At Google's I/O 2021 conference, the company announced what sounds like a  complete re-launch of Wear OS, with Samsung as a major partner. Wear OS's biggest remaining hardware vendor is fashion company Fossil Group, and in a weekend  interview with CNET, the company shed a bit more light on the project.
 "Premium" seems to be the name of the game for the revamped Wear OS. Fossil is currently charging $350 for the Fossil Gen 5 LTE, while an LTE-equipped Apple Watch costs closer to $500. When the new Wear OS comes out, we'll probably see watches more in line with Apple's pricing. CNET came away from the interview saying, "The upcoming wave of Android-compatible watches will be entirely new, with new chips focused on faster performance and better battery life."
 Many  sites  concluded that existing watches would not be updated from the old Wear OS to the new Wear OS. Fossil responded by sending out a statement that didn't deny the speculation and only said that "future upgrade plans are still being developed." Samsung has said that Tizen watches won't be upgraded to Wear OS, but that's a completely different operating system.
 It would not surprise me to hear that the new Wear OS will be a clean break from the old Wear OS. If the new Wear OS, supposedly developed "jointly with Samsung," is targeting a modern hardware spec, it would be challenging to bring the existing devices forward. Wear OS has lacked a quality SoC option for some time, and while existing Fossil and other Wear OS watches might seem to only be a few years (or a few months) old, the Qualcomm chips they use have been obsolete for years.
     Even devices like the Fossil Gen 5 LTE, which launched a few months ago, have the Snapdragon Wear 3100, which uses Cortex A7 CPUs and a 28 nm manufacturing process. Qualcomm's gross neglect of the wearables space means this 2021 watch features state-of-the-art technology from 2014, so imagine running around today with a Snapdragon 801-powered Galaxy S5 and expecting updates.
 On the other hand, Samsung's Exynos division has not completely abandoned the smartwatch SoC market, and the upcoming Galaxy Watch 4—expected to be the launch device for the revamped Wear OS— is rumored to come with a 5 nm Samsung SoC. If we're dating these chips by the manufacturing process, there are  seven years of technology improvements between them. That's the kind of hardware change that won't lead to many devices being upgraded, since designing for something that slow would hold the platform back.
 In 2020, Qualcomm did offer its first significant smartwatch upgrade in six years, the 12 nm  Wear 4100, but manufacturers have  mostly ignored that chip. The 4100 will be a year old at the end of the month, and it looks like this was a one-off update from Qualcomm and not the start of a yearly upgrade cycle that could lead to a viable smartwatch hardware platform. Fossil says it has "some pretty major hardware upgrades" coming for the new Wear OS; hopefully, the company is talking about more than the Wear 4100.
 Over the past six years, we've seen Wear OS hardware makers slowly starve to death due to a lack of access to quality SoCs. Samsung entering the ecosystem with its own chips solves that problem for the company, but will any other hardware makers get access? Samsung has  occasionally sold Exynos chips to competing vendors, but it has never made a real business out of it, and it has never sold smartphone chips. Google says Wear OS is still open to hardware manufacturers, but if only Samsung gets Samsung chips and other Wear OS OEMs like Fossil get Qualcomm chips, the competition might as well close up shop now. This is how Samsung sold Tizen watches in the past, so maybe Samsung and Google are OK with it.
title: 不要指望改进的Google /三星版本的许多佩戴操作系统更新
title_en: Don’t expect many Wear OS updates to the revamped Google/Samsung version

立即购买，稍后支付狂热不会随着更多消费者寻求信用卡的替代品来资助购买。
 这些购买并不是奢侈品，如Pelotons（AHEM，肯定）或珠宝可能会对在线治疗自己的珠宝。一家新的金融公司是帮助消费者金融的大票商品，被认为是“必须拥有”的大票商品比“很高兴”。它刚刚筹集了1400万美元的资金，以帮助它提升这一目标。
 Neal Desai（辛烷值贷款的前CFO）和詹姆斯·舒勒（作为一个高中学家参加Y Combinator的加速计划）在2019年7月成立了纽约市的Kafene。这对目标是通过满足什么来促进金融包容性它描述为“传统贷方留下的消费者”。
  更具体地说，KAFENE专注于帮助消费者在650次购买零售项目之后的信用评分，如家具，电器和电子产品，立即购买，稍后支付（BNPL）模型。考虑到这是“对次次审判”，“德莱说。
 全球创始人资本和第三次优质企业联合领导了这一轮，其中还包括从瓦尔，公司.Co，赫尔曼资本，Gaingels，共和国实验室，不相关企业和FJ实验室的参与。
 “从历史上看，如果你可以访问信用，你可以去银行或使用信用卡，”第三个Prime的Wes Barton告诉TechCrunch。 “但如果你有一些意想不到的费用，并且不得不错过银行的付款，会有影响，你可能会陷入债务陷阱。”
 KAFENE的“灵活所有权”模型旨在使其发生在消费者身上。如果出于某种原因，有人必须在付款上没收，Kafene来拿起该物品，而客户则不再有义务支付其前进的义务。 
它的工作方式是Kafene代表消费者的商家购买产品，并在12个月内租给他们。如果他们制定所有付款，他们都拥有该项目。如果他们早些时候制作它们，他们会得到“显着的”折扣，如果他们不能，Kafene会回收物品并拿贷款损失。
  这是一个现代化的租盘 - 一个中心，为劣质产品收取更多金钱，黛贝相信。
 “这也是信用卡的优秀产品，该市场的规模庞大，”巴顿说。 “我们希望及时采取一大堆信用卡业务，并使消费者在任何时间点戒掉的灵活性，如果你愿意，免费飞行。”
 这种灵活性，KAFENE索赔，通过在销售点提供更广泛的消费者选择来促进金融包容性。
 这也有助于人们根据Desai的说法提高他们的信用评分，因为如果他们在12个月的期限早期购买贷款，他们的信用评分将上涨，因为Kafene将它们作为一个正面的付款人报告。
 “在任何情况下他们没有偷取物品的情况下，他们的信用评分有所提高，”他说。 “即使他们最终返回它，因为他们买不起。从长远来看，他们可以获得更好的信用评分，以获得传统贷款产品。“
 Kafene于2019年12月推出了其融资产品的测试版，然后由于Covid-19大流行，3月份不得不暂停。本公司从3月到2020年3月到6月开始“休眠”，去年7月重新启动了β。 
到10月，KAFENE停止了商家的所有入学，因为它有更多的需求，它可以处理 - 由于Covid-19大流行，更多的人在经济上紧张的更多人。 2021年3月，该公司在商品量下每月处理约200万美元。  凭借其新的首都，Kafene计划在全国范围内显着扩展其现有的租赁融资业务，以及推出直接消费者的虚拟租赁卡。
note_en:
The buy now, pay later frenzy isn’t going anywhere as more consumers seek alternatives to credit cards to fund purchases.
 And those purchases aren’t exclusive to luxuries such as Pelotons (ahem, Affirm) or jewelry someone might be treating themselves to online. A new fintech company is out to help consumers finance big-ticket items that are considered more “must have” than “nice to have.” And it’s just raised $14 million in Series A funding to help it advance on that goal.
 Neal Desai (former CFO of Octane Lending) and James Schuler (who participated in Y Combinator’s accelerator program as a high schooler) founded New York City-based  Kafene in July 2019. The pair’s goal is to promote financial inclusion by meeting the needs of what it describes as the “consumers that are left behind by traditional lenders.”
  More specifically, Kafene is focused on helping consumers with credit scores below 650 purchase retail items such as furniture, appliances and electronics with its buy now, pay later (BNPL) model. Consider it an “Affirm for the subprime,” says Desai.
 Global Founders Capital and Third Prime Ventures co-led the round, which also included participation from Valar, Company.co, Hermann Capital, Gaingels, Republic Labs, Uncorrelated Ventures and FJ labs.
 “Historically, if you could access credit, you could go to the bank or use a credit card,” Third Prime’s Wes Barton told TechCrunch. “But if you had some unexpected expense, and had to miss a payment with the bank, there would be repercussions and you could fall into a debt trap.”
 Kafene’s “flexible ownership” model is designed to not let that happen to a consumer. If for some reason, someone has to forfeit on a payment, Kafene comes to pick up the item and the customer is no longer under obligation to pay for it moving forward.
 The way it works is that Kafene buys the product from a merchant on a consumers’ behalf and rents it back to them over 12 months. If they make all payments, they own the item. If they make them earlier, they get a “significant” discount, and if they can’t, Kafene reclaims the item and takes the loan loss.
  It’s a modern take on Rent-A-Center, which charges more money for inferior products, Desai believes.
 “This is also a superior product to credit cards, and the size of that market is massive,” Barton said. “We want to take a huge chunk of credit card business in time, and give consumers the flexibility to quit at any point in time, and fly free, if you will.”
 Such flexibility, Kafene claims, helps promote financial inclusion by giving a wider range of consumers options to alternative forms of credit at the point of sale.
 It also helps people boost their credit scores, according to Desai, because if they buy out of the loan earlier than the 12-month term, their credit score goes up because Kafene reports them as a positive payer.
 “In any situation where they don’t steal the item, their credit score improves,” he said. “Even if they end up returning it because they can’t afford it. In the long run, they can have a better credit score to qualify for a traditional loan product.”
 Kafene rolled out a beta of its financing product in December of 2019 and then had to pause in March due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The company essentially “hibernated” from March to June 2020 and re-launched out of beta last July.
 By October, Kafene stopped all enrollment with merchants because it had more demand that it could handle — largely fueled by more people being financially strained due to the COVID-19 pandemic. In March 2021, the company was handling about $2 million a month in merchandise volume.
 With its new capital, Kafene plans to significantly scale its existing lease-to-own financing business nationally, as well as to launch a direct-to-consumer virtual lease card.
title: Kafene筹集了1400万美元，立即购买，后来支付次级消费者
title_en: Kafene raises $14M to offer buy now, pay later to the subprime consumer

旨在保护数百万美国人的遗传隐私，专注于消费者DNA数据库，如23andme，祖先，吉丁巴和家庭特征，所有这些法律都令人难以让人们上传他们的遗传信息并使用它来与遥远的亲戚联系追踪他们的家谱。虽然受欢迎 -  23和ME拥有超过300万用户，而GedMatch超过一百万 - 许多人没有意识到一些这些平台与第三方共享遗传数据，从制药行业和科学家到执法机构。
 当法律执行通过被称为法医遗传遗传学的技术（FGGS）使用时，官员可以上传在犯罪现场发现的DNA证据，以便在可能的嫌疑人上建立联系，最着名的例子是2018年识别金州杀手。这次锯调查人员在1980年谋杀案中拍摄的DNA样本与牛仔杀手联系起来，随后识别嫌疑人的遥远亲属 - 这导致约瑟夫詹姆斯·迪安格罗逮捕的关键突破。
  虽然执法机构在利用消费者DNA数据库获得了成功的措施，但在刑事调查中援助，但隐私倡导者长期以来警告这些平台的危险。这些DNA配置文件不仅可以帮助追溯父亲，而且他们持有的巨大遗传数据途径可以透露一个人对各种疾病的倾向，预测成瘾和药物反应，甚至公司使用他们认为的人的图像好像。
 祖先和23andme使他们的遗传数据库在没有逮捕令的情况下关闭了执法，吉丁（2019年12月由犯罪现场DNA公司收购）和ForicalTredna先前与调查人员分享了他们的数据库。
 为了确保被告及其亲属的遗传隐私，马里兰州将于10月1日开始，要求执法在使用遗传遗传学之前，在使用遗传遗传学之前，将法官的签字进行限制，并将限制谋杀，绑架和人口贩运等严重罪行。它还表示，调查人员只能使用明确告诉用户他们的信息可以用来调查犯罪的数据库。
 在蒙大拿州，在新规则有点窄，除非用户豁免其隐私权，否则法律执行将需要一个逮捕令。
 法律“展示了政治频统的人们发现了法律执法使用消费者遗传数据寒冷，有关和隐私入侵者，”马里兰大学法律教授娜塔莉拉姆说。 “我希望看到更多州在未来拥抱本执法技术的强劲监管。” 
这些法律的引入也被隐私权倡导者圆满欢迎，包括电子前沿基金会。 Jennifer Lynch，EFF的监督诉讼主任，将限制描述为“朝着正确的方向上的步骤”，但呼吁更多的国家 - 和联邦政府 - 进一步崩溃。
 “我们的遗传数据太敏感，很重要，无法留给私营公司的突发事件，以保护它和肆无忌惮的执法自行决定，”林奇说。
 “像Gedmatch和Familytredna这样的公司允许甚至鼓励执法搜索。因此，执法人员越来越多地访问全国刑事调查中的这些数据库。“
 23andme的发言人告诉TechCrunch：“我们完全支持提供具有更强隐私保护的消费者的立法。事实上，我们正在努力在许多国家的立法中增加消费者遗传隐私保护。客户隐私和透明度是指导23ANDME响应法律要求和维护客户信任的方法的核心原则。我们密切审查所有执法和监管要求，我们只会遵守法院命令，传票，搜索权证或其他我们决定在法律上有效的请求。迄今为止，我们尚未将任何客户信息发布到执法部门。“
 祖先表示，它与马里兰州的立法者合作，制定了一项法律，“这在确保消费者的隐私”中实现了公共政策目标。“
 “保护客户的隐私并成为他们数据的好管家是祖先的最优先事项，我们不会自愿与执法人员合作。一家公司发言人表示，除非有效的法律程序，否则祖先不会与执法共享任何信息，除非有效的法律程序，例如法院命令或搜查权证。“
 GedMatch和FamilyTreDNA，其中默认情况下，将用户加入执法人员搜索，告诉纽约时报，他们没有计划根据新规定改变其同意的现有政策。
note_en:
The new laws, which aim to safeguard the genetic privacy of millions of Americans, focus on consumer DNA databases, such as  23andMe,  Ancestry,  GEDmatch and FamilyTreeDNA, all of which let people upload their genetic information and use it to connect with distant relatives and trace their family tree.  While popular — 23andMe has more than three million users, and GEDmatch more than one million — many are unaware that some of these platforms share genetic data with third parties, from the pharmaceutical industry and scientists to law enforcement agencies.
 When used by law enforcement through a technique known as forensic genetic genealogy searching (FGGS), officers can upload DNA evidence found at a crime scene to make connections on possible suspects,  the most famous example being the identification of the Golden State Killer in 2018. This saw investigators upload a DNA sample taken at the time of a 1980 murder linked to the serial killer into GEDmatch and subsequently identify distant relatives of the suspect — a critical breakthrough that led to the arrest of Joseph James DeAngelo.
  While law enforcement agencies have seen success in using consumer DNA databases to aid with criminal investigations, privacy advocates have long warned of the dangers of these platforms. Not only can these DNA profiles help trace distant ancestors, but the vast troves of genetic data they hold can divulge a person’s propensity for various diseases, predict addiction and drug response, and even be used by companies to create images of what they think a person looks like.
 Ancestry and 23andMe have kept their genetic databases closed to law enforcement without a warrant, GEDmatch (which was acquired by a crime scene DNA company in December 2019) and FamilyTreeDNA have previously shared their database with investigators.
 To ensure the genetic privacy of the accused and their relatives, Maryland will, starting October 1,  require law enforcement to get a judge’s sign-off before using genetic genealogy, and will limit its use to serious crimes like murder, kidnapping, and human trafficking. It also says that investigators can only use databases that explicitly tell users that their information could be used to investigate crimes.
 In Montana, where the new rules are somewhat narrower,  law enforcement would need a warrant before using a DNA database unless the users waived their rights to privacy.
 The laws “demonstrate that people across the political spectrum find law enforcement use of consumer genetic data chilling, concerning and privacy-invasive,” said Natalie Ram, a law professor at the University of Maryland. “I hope to see more states embrace robust regulation of this law enforcement technique in the future.”
 The introduction of these laws has also been roundly welcomed by privacy advocates,  including the Electronic Frontier Foundation. Jennifer Lynch, surveillance litigation director at the EFF, described the restrictions as a “step in the right direction,” but called for more states — and the federal government — to crack down further on FGGS.
 “Our genetic data is too sensitive and important to leave it up to the whims of private companies to protect it and the unbridled discretion of law enforcement to search it,” Lynch said.
 “Companies like GEDmatch and FamilyTreeDNA have allowed and even encouraged law enforcement searches. Because of this, law enforcement officers are increasingly accessing these databases in criminal investigations across the country.”
 A spokesperson for 23andMe told TechCrunch: “We fully support legislation that provides consumers with stronger privacy protections. In fact we are working on legislation in a number of states to increase consumer genetic privacy protections. Customer privacy and transparency are core principles that guide 23andMe’s approach to responding to legal requests and maintaining customer trust. We closely scrutinize all law enforcement and regulatory requests and we will only comply with court orders, subpoenas, search warrants or other requests that we determine are legally valid. To date we have not released any customer information to law enforcement.”
 Ancestry said it worked with lawmakers in Maryland to craft a law “that achieves their public policy goals while ensuring the privacy of our consumers.”
 “Protecting our customers’ privacy and being good stewards of their data is Ancestry’s highest priority and we do not voluntarily work with law enforcement. Ancestry will not share any information with law enforcement unless compelled to by valid legal process, such as a court order or search warrant,” a company spokesperson said.
 GEDmatch and FamilyTreeDNA, both of which opt users into law enforcement searches by default, told  the New York Times that they have no plans to change their existing policies around user consent in response to the new regulation.
title: 马里兰州和蒙大拿州限制了警察进入DNA数据库
title_en: Maryland and Montana are restricting police access to DNA databases

Apple在开发人员地址期间注意到制作伟大AR应用的最困难部分之一是创建3D模型的过程。这些可能需要数小时和数千美元。
 使用Apple的新工具，开发人员将能够使用一系列使用iPhone（或iPad或DSLR，如果他们更喜欢）以捕获来自所有角度的对象的2D图像，包括底部。
  然后，在麦斯科斯镜头上使用对象捕获API，它只需要几行代码来生成3D模型，Apple解释说。
  首先，开发人员将在RealityKit中开始新的摄影测量会话，该会话指向他们捕获图像的文件夹。然后，他们将调用过程函数以期望的详细级别生成3D模型。对象捕获允许开发人员为AR快速外观生成优化的USDZ文件 - 让开发人员在iPhone和iPad上的应用程序或网站中添加虚拟，3D对象的系统。 3D模型也可以将3D模型添加到Xcode中的现实作曲器中的AR场景中。
 Apple表示，像Wayfair，Etsy和其他人这样的开发人员正在使用对象捕获来创建现实世界对象的3D模型 - 这是一个迹象，即在线购物即将获得大型AR升级。
 例如，Wayfair正在使用对象捕获来为其制造商开发工具，因此它们可以创建其商品的虚拟表示。这将允许Wayfair客户能够在今天的AR中预览更多产品。
  此外，Apple注意到包括Maxon和Unity的开发人员正在使用对象捕获来在3D内容创建应用程序中创建3D内容，例如Cinema 4D和Unity Mars。 
RealityKit 2中的其他更新包括自定义着色器，使开发人员更加控制渲染管道以微调AR对象的外观和感受; 资产的动态装载; 构建自己的实体组件系统的能力，以组织AR场景中的资产; 以及创建玩家控制的角色的能力，因此用户可以在基于RealityKit的游戏中跳跃，规模和探索AR世界。  一位开发人员Mikko Haapoja of Shopify，一直在尝试新技术（见下文）并分享一些现实世界测试，他通过Twitter使用iPhone 12 Max拍摄对象。  想要测试它的开发人员可以利用Apple的示例应用程序，并在他们的Mac上安装蒙特利以尝试一下。  Apple＆＃39; S对象捕获在菠萝上。 我的一个最喜欢测试摄影测量的东西。 这是使用原始细节设置处理的。  Apple＆＃39;对象捕获是真正的交易。 我印象深刻。 很高兴看到@shopify商家可以接受这个
note_en:
Apple noted during its developer address that one of the most difficult parts of making great AR apps was the process of creating 3D models. These could take hours and thousands of dollars.
 With Apple’s new tools, developers will be able take a series of pictures using just an iPhone (or iPad or DSLR, if they prefer) to capture 2D images of an object from all angles, including the bottom.
  Then, using the Object Capture API on macOS Monterey, it only takes a few lines of code to generate the 3D model, Apple explained.
  To begin, developers would start a new photogrammetry session in RealityKit that points to the folder where they’ve captured the images. Then, they would call the process function to generate the 3D model at the desired level of detail. Object Capture allows developers to generate the USDZ files optimized for  AR Quick Look — the system that lets developers add virtual, 3D objects in apps or websites on iPhone and iPad. The 3D models can also be added to AR scenes in Reality Composer in Xcode.
 Apple said developers like Wayfair, Etsy and others are using Object Capture to create 3D models of real-world objects — an indication that online shopping is about to get a big AR upgrade.
 Wayfair, for example, is using Object Capture to develop tools for their manufacturers so they can create a virtual representation of their merchandise. This will allow Wayfair customers to be able to preview more products in AR than they could today.
  In addition, Apple noted developers including Maxon and Unity are using Object Capture for creating 3D content within 3D content creation apps, such as Cinema 4D and Unity MARS.
 Other  updates in RealityKit 2 include custom shaders that give developers more control over the rendering pipeline to fine tune the look and feel of AR objects; dynamic loading for assets; the ability to build your own Entity Component System to organize the assets in your AR scene; and the ability to create player-controlled characters so users can jump, scale and explore AR worlds in RealityKit-based games.
 One developer,  Mikko Haapoja of Shopify, has been trying out the new technology (see below) and shared some real-world tests where he shot objects using an iPhone 12 Max via Twitter.
 Developers who want to test it for themselves can leverage  Apple’s sample app and install Monterey on their Mac to try it out.
 Apple's Object Capture on a Pineapple. One of my fav things to test Photogrammetry against. This was processed using the RAW detail setting.
 Apple's Object Capture is the real deal. I'm impressed. Excited to see where  @Shopify merchants could take this
title: Apple的RealityKit 2允许开发人员使用iPhone照片创建AR的3D模型
title_en: Apple’s RealityKit 2 allows developers to create 3D models for AR using iPhone photos

  “卡内基梅龙的数十年在艾和机器人中的研究和教育的长期领导促进了匹兹堡地区的创新生态系统，在那里创业，创造力和摆放相交，”贾尼安告诉科技。 “这些新兴技术正在改变我们的农场方式，使数百万来学习一种新语言，导致比赛来​​开发自驾驶车辆，甚至去月球。我们致力于培养匹兹堡的公民，参与这些创新的经济利益，因为它们继续改变我们的世界。“
 匹兹堡是一个奇妙的创业生态系统，通过利用其令人难以置信的大学，欢迎的地方政府和经验丰富的投资者来迅速增长。据千里之外，匹兹堡长期以来一直致力于通过研发的硅谷的成功。对于TechCrunch，Pittsburgh代表了一个利用其当地资产的城市，以帮助建立在该地区的公司。我们最近在Duolingo发表了一个深刻的简介，突出了当地匹兹堡生态系统如何帮助将启动转化为十亿美元的公司。
 TechCrunch硬件编辑Brian加热器正在采访Jahanian。加热器是塑造和编程TechCrunch过去的事件的关键，包括会话：机器人，CMU的基石纪律。

note_en:
President Jahanian is an outspoken advocate for science and innovation, making him perfect to headline our event focused on startup activity in Pittsburg, PA. Speaking to a House committee in April, Jahanian identified recent trends shaping the research and development throughout the United States. Among those mentioned was a widening opportunity gap and rising economic inequality — topics that are also fundamentally shaping startups in Silicon Valley, Pittsburgh, and elsewhere.
  “Carnegie Mellon’s decades-long leadership in research and education in AI and robotics has catalyzed an innovation ecosystem in the Pittsburgh region where entrepreneurship, creativity and placemaking intersect,” Jahanian told TechCrunch. “These emerging technologies are changing the way we farm, enabling millions to learn a new language, leading the race to develop self-driving vehicles, and even going to the moon. We are committed to empowering citizens across Pittsburgh to take part in the economic benefits of these innovations as they continue to transform our world.”
 Pittsburgh is a fantastic startup ecosystem quickly growing by leveraging its incredible universities, a welcoming local government, and experienced investors. Though thousands of miles away, Pittsburgh has long contributed to Silicon Valley’s success through research and development. To TechCrunch, Pittsburgh represents a city leveraging its local assets to help build companies in and out of the area. We recently  published a deep profile on Duolingo that highlights how the local Pittsburgh ecosystem helped turn the startup into a billion dollar company.
 TechCrunch Hardware Editor Brian Heater is interviewing Jahanian. Heater was key in shaping and programming TechCrunch’s past events including Sessions: Robotics, a cornerstone discipline at CMU.
 TechCrunch City Spotlight: Pittsburgh features talks from Jahanian, local startups, and local leaders. More names will be announced in the coming weeks.
 We’re still looking for startups to participate in the event. It’s free to register and participating in networking and watch the event ( click here to register). It’s also free to apply to pitch your startup at the event ( click here to apply). We’re looking for early stage companies from the greater Pittsburgh area that can give a two minute pitch to a panel of local venture capitalists in exchange for feedback.

霍尼韦尔长期以来一直与CQ合作，并在去年投资公司。这里的想法是将霍尼韦尔的硬件专业知识与CQ的软件焦点相结合，以构建两家公司称之为“世界上最常用的量子计算机以及一套全套量子软件，包括第一和最先进的量子操作系统。”
 合并的公司（或“组合”，因为公司的新闻稿称之为）预计将在2021年第三季度完成交易。霍尼韦尔主席兼首席执行官Darius Adamczyk将成为新公司的主席。 CQ创始人兼首席执行官伊利亚斯汗将成为首席执行官，目前霍尼韦尔量子解决方案总统托尼Uttley将留在新公司的角色。
  这里的想法是霍尼韦尔脱离了HQ，并将其与CQC结合在一起形成新公司，同时仍然在其领导和财务中发挥作用。霍尼韦尔将拥有新公司的大多数股份，投资270美元至3亿美元。它还将与新公司进行长期协议，以在其量子硬件的核心构建离子陷阱。 CQ的股东将拥有45％的新公司。
  “新公司将拥有业内最优秀的人才，世界上最多的量子计算机，第一和最先进的量子操作系统，以及全面，硬件无关的软件，将推动量子计算行业的未来，”说adamczyk。 “通过提供支持该部门爆炸性增长所需的关键全球基础设施，新公司将极为良好地在量子计算行业内创造价值。”
 这些公司认为，大规模投资需要支持成功的量子业务，并为结合硬件和软件的客户提供一站式商店。通过将两家公司结合起来，他们注意到，他们将能够在其专门领域的领域建立各自的领导地位，并在其业务范围内扩大其业务，同时也加速其R＆amp; D和产品路线图。
 “既然我们在2018年首次宣布霍尼韦尔的量子业务，我们已经听到许多投资者，这些投资者渴望直接在这一令人兴奋和动态行业的前沿直接投资 - 现在，他们将能够这样做，”Adamczyk说。 “新公司将为我们提供最佳大道，以便在规模上船上新的，多样化的资本来源，这将有助于推动快速增长。”
 CQ于2014年推出，现在拥有大约150名员工。该公司总计7280万美元，其中包括4500万美元，该公司去年12月宣布。霍尼韦尔，IBM Ventures，JSR Corporation，Serendiquity，Alvarium Investments和Staripot Holdings在最后一轮投资 - 这也意味着IBM使用不同的技术，但在许多方面，直接与新公司竞争，现在拥有A（小）部分。
note_en:
Honeywell has long partnered with CQ and invested in the company last year, too. The idea here is to combine Honeywell’s hardware expertise with CQ’s software focus to build what the two companies call “the world’s highest-performing quantum computer and a full suite of quantum software, including the first and most advanced quantum operating system.”
 The merged companies (or ‘combination,’ as the companies’ press releases calls it) expect the deal to be completed in the third quarter of 2021. Honeywell Chairman and CEO Darius Adamczyk will become the chairman of the new company. CQ founder and CEO Ilyas Khan will become the CEO and current Honeywell Quantum Solutions President Tony Uttley will remain in this role at the new company.
  The idea here is for Honeywell to spin off HQS and combine it with CQC to form a new company, while still playing a role in its leadership and finances. Honeywell will own a majority stake in the new company and invest between $270 and $300 million. It will also have a long-term agreement with the new company to build the ion traps at the core of its quantum hardware. CQ’s shareholders will own 45% of the new company.
  “The new company will have the best talent in the industry, the world’s highest-performing quantum computer, the first and most advanced quantum operating system, and comprehensive, hardware-agnostic software that will drive the future of the quantum computing industry,” said Adamczyk. “The new company will be extremely well positioned to create value in the near-term within the quantum computing industry by offering the critical global infrastructure needed to support the sector’s explosive growth.”
 The companies argue that a successful quantum business will need to be supported by large-scale investments and offer a one-stop shop for customers that combines hardware and software. By combining the two companies now, they note, they’ll be able to build on their respective leadership positions in their areas of expertise and scale their businesses while also accelerate their R&D and product roadmaps.
 “Since we first announced Honeywell’s  quantum business in 2018, we have heard from many investors who have been eager to invest directly in our leading technologies at the forefront of this exciting and dynamic industry – now, they will be able to do so,” Adamczyk said. “The new company will provide the best avenue for us to onboard new, diverse sources of capital at scale that will help drive rapid growth.”
 CQ launched in 2014 and now has about 150 employees. The company raised a total of $72.8 million, including a $45 million round, which it announced last December. Honeywell, IBM Ventures, JSR Corporation, Serendipity Capital, Alvarium Investments and Talipot Holdings invested in this last round — which also means that IBM, which uses a different technology but, in many ways, directly competes with the new company, now owns a (small) part of it.
title: 霍尼韦尔和剑桥量子形成了合资企业，建造一个新的全堆栈量子业务
title_en: Honeywell and Cambridge Quantum form joint venture to build a new full-stack quantum business

Todd Klein是革命成长的合伙人，这是一个投资于增长阶段的公司的VC基金。在他20年的职业生涯中，托德一直参与媒体，消费，技术，教育和医疗部门的150多家创业和增长级公司融资和建设。
  与此同时，消费者越来越多地希望与与他们不断增长的环境价值保持一致的品牌，而不是忽略他们消费的气候后果。即使没有规则作为棍子，消费者需求现在也担任胡萝卜，以提高可持续性对公共公司议程的影响。
 初创公司已经遵循了衣服。投资者今天将可持续发展视为任何商业模式的重要支柱，正在寻找“从一开始就”获得它“的企业家，以建立和规模下一代公司。对蓬勃发展感兴趣的初创企业不能将可持续性视为事后的经文，并应准备使用可持续增长计划进入公众。
   那么创始人需要到达哪些内容，以证明他们在可持续发展方面处于正确的轨道？以下是投资者正在寻找的五个属性。
  对于任何公司来说，任何公司都可以尊重客户的需求和需求是相当容易的，但是将技术堆栈证明公司实际上倾向于客户反馈并满足这些期望，这是一项挑战性。
 投资者现在预计初创公司将拥有平台和解决方案 - 社交听力渠道，关系管理工具，测量计划和审查论坛 - 使他们能够听到和对客户的需求。如果没有适当的通信工具和使用它们的实际人，因此您的环保努力可能似乎只是唇部服务。
 借用温度的例子，为寒冷，易腐食品和药品的出货量制造可回收的绝缘包装解决方案。 Packager之间的直接关系像锻炼和最终消费者一样是不可见的。但正如我们正在研究公司的投资，其一些生命科学客户告诉我们，他们从最终用户收到的评论 - 每天两次接受药物的人。另一家供应商的包装要求他们访问回收商进行处理，这是一个真正的痛苦点，导致他们考虑切换到不同的药物。 
革命成长决定在直接在措施中直接查看其客户反馈循环后将温电公司添加为投资组合公司：最终用户请求知情的产品开发，证明市场需求和客户对可持续发展正面的需求。此第一手示例演示了投资者，包装制造商，生命科学公司和最终用户现在如何在一种关系中互连，同时强调最终用户的反馈如何连接可持续产品开发的点。
  在过去的几年里，我们已经看到千禧一代和Gen Z消费者在可持续性努力中要求透明度。随着这些世代在购买力的增加，投资者将寻找初创公司，使其承诺尽可能透明地使其成为透明的透明度，以满足精明的消费者需求。
 对于许多VCS来说，对可持续发展目标的公共承诺是您的初创公司正致力于成为下一代公司的标志。投资者将寻找有思想的目标，并清楚地了解贵公司的机构和影响力，这是S.M.A.R.T（具体，可衡量，可实现，现实和及时）。他们还会期望有关进展的定期报告。
 虽然公司的管理层建立了这些目标，但其董事会应该在推动目标方面发挥作用的落后作用，使得在轨道上保持领导和设定竞争对手，因此高管了解正在超越正面EBIDTA的标准评估。
 采取这些步骤将确保目标是负责任和雄心勃勃的同时也持有本公司对消费者和利益相关者负责，以便完成举措。
  即使是最好的可持续发展目标也会在没有强大的文化的情况下取消满足，旨在保证领导和员工对齐。可持续发展必须在初创企业的文化中加入 - 从上下和自下而下 - 如果不是，那就有很多股权。
 另一个革命增长组合公司，全球金融化革命性启动塔拉展示了年轻公司如何用目的驱动的价值观筹集其文化。虽然Tala的使命是向Unbank的信贷提供信贷，但该公司认为，消费者的最佳利益始终是第一。在2019年假日季节期间，Tala与企业造成促进消费的企业对比，而是敦促肯尼亚的客户无法拿出贷款，在缺乏运作的信用局和贷款堆叠数据库中保护它们免受掠夺性的不受管制的贷款。这种前瞻性的方法最终使Tala的客户及其充满活力的数字贷款行业。 
除了确定他们的代表，我们的许多投资组合企业面临挑战的挑战。人们可以选择他们想要工作的地方，以及具有内在动机的人 - 例如对环境的担忧 - 如果他们的雇主不分享他们的价值观会感到不舒服。与丢失明星表演者的人类成本相比，监管风险与客户疲惫不堪，他们寻求其他与其价值观变得更好地对齐的其他工作文化。
 明确的价值观系统应将可持续性纳入决策过程中，使员工明显并使雇员遵循。
  公司不仅由自己的倡议评判 - 他们也被他们的合作伙伴判断。由于初创公司建立新的关系或扩展以与新供应商合作，投资者将热衷于知道这些外部缔约方与他们所说的可持续性哲学对齐。
 在与另一家公司公开参与之前，启动应衡量每个新供应商的声誉，包括洞察的就业实践。乘坐地中海快速休闲餐厅Cava或健康风格的沙拉中心环，革命增长组合公司;没有因非人汶政策的农场来源蛋白质。如果公司不了解这些因素，他们的客户最终将让他们知道，并且可能使他们持有对监督负责。
 这意味着它：如果您的伙伴关系和供应商关系的图表在纽约时报的首页上打印出来，您会对世界显示的内容感到满意吗？如今，各种规模的公司都被消费者，员工，潜在合作伙伴和媒体持有更高的标准。它不再可能在雷达下飞行与对公司可持续发展目标的关系。因此，难以看看您的供应商和合作伙伴生态系统，并清楚地通过您的业务的每一个延伸将您的绿色愿景带到生活中。
  金融现实主义承认公司可以想要做得好，但除非他们有经济学，否则他们不会生存以产生影响。对于大多数初创公司，从金融现实主义开始作为一种心态和渐进主义，作为一种方法，将成为成功的关键，使所有企业能够为更具弹性的星球做出贡献。对于优先级的初创公司，优先涉及产品或服务的环保业务实践，这种策略可以防止善良成为伟大的敌人。该职位的创始人可以致力于逐步的可持续发展计划，而不是期待过夜转型。投资者了解努力迎接绿色目标和保持灯光之间的微妙平衡。
 寻求建立一个不仅采用环保实践的企业家，而且在其心脏上也有可持续发展可能需要考虑在利基产业或市场上的价格，这对今天的价格较低并准备好的解决方案。一旦该解决方案牢固建立，业务就可以建立在他们创造的内容上，而不是与不扩展的东西大 - 而且失败的东西。如果没有初始客户的价值和爱你正在做的事情，你就不会到达更大的戏剧。 
随着公共和私营部门继续解决气候危机，可持续性将越来越多地成为一个授权而不是选择，资金越来越多地流向解决潜在环境问题的初创企业。遗憾的是，为满足可持续发展需求的公司而导致“绿色洗手” - 绿色营销的欺骗性地利用劝说消费者公司的产品，目标和政策是环保的。
 绿色洗手已经强迫投资者，以超越仅仅是行动的言论。随着我们走向更可持续的未来，追求VC资金的初创公司将需要向投资者证明可持续性是整个组织的优先事项，使其与责任和具体实施活动的责任和具体示例对齐他们的外联，公共承诺和文化。即使这些例子只是迈向更大目标的措施，他们也将向投资者和客户展示初创公司准备好明天贡献更加绿色和更好。
note_en:
Todd Klein is a partner at  Revolution Growth, a VC fund that invests in growth-stage companies. During his 20-year career, Todd has been involved in financing and building over 150 venture and growth-stage companies in the media, consumer, technology, education and healthcare sectors.
  At the same time, consumers are  increasingly looking to do business with brands that align with their growing environmental values, rather than ignoring the climate consequences of their consumption. Even without regulation as a stick, consumer demand is now serving as a carrot to increase sustainability’s impact on public companies’ agendas.
 Startups have already followed suit. Investors today view sustainability as an important pillar of any business model and are looking for entrepreneurs who “get it” from the beginning to build and scale next-generation companies. Startups interested in thriving cannot treat sustainability as an afterthought and should be prepared to enter the public eye with a plan for sustainable growth.
   So what exactly do founders need to put in place to demonstrate that they’re on the right track when it comes to sustainability? Here are five attributes that investors are looking for.
  It’s fairly easy for any company to claim that it understands customers’ wants and needs, but it’s challenging to have the tech stack in place to prove a company actually listens to customer feedback and meets those expectations.
 Investors now expect startups to have both platforms and solutions — social listening channels, relationship management tools, surveying programs and review forums — that allow them to hear and act on the needs of their customers. Without the proper communications tools and actual people using them, your eco-friendly efforts will likely appear to be merely lip service.
 Take the example of  TemperPack, which manufactures recyclable insulated packaging solutions for shipments of cold, perishable foods and pharmaceuticals. The direct relationship between a packager like TemperPack and the end consumer is often invisible. But as we were looking into investing in the company, some of its life sciences customers told us about comments they had received from end users — people who were receiving medicine twice per day. Another supplier’s packaging required them to visit a recycler for disposal, a real-world pain point that was causing them to consider switching to a different medication.
 Revolution Growth decided to add TemperPack as a portfolio company after directly seeing its customer feedback loop in action: End-user requests informed product development, proving both a market need and customer demand on the sustainability front. This firsthand example demonstrates how an investor, a packaging maker, a life sciences company and an end user are now interconnected in one relationship while underscoring how end-user feedback can connect the dots for sustainable product development.
  Over the past several years, we have seen millennials and Gen Z consumers  demand transparency in sustainability efforts. As these generations grow in purchasing power, investors will look for startups that make their commitments to eco-friendly goals as transparent as possible to satisfy shrewd consumer needs.
 For many VCs, making public commitments to sustainability goals is a sign that your startup is working toward becoming a next-generation company. Investors will look for goals that are thoughtful, with a clear understanding of where your company will have agency and influence, and that are S.M.A.R.T (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic and Timely). They will also expect regular reports on progress.
 Although a company’s management establishes these goals, its board should play a behind-the-scenes role in driving the goals forward, keeping leadership on track and setting the playing field so executives understand that they’re being evaluated on criteria transcending positive EBIDTA.
 Taking these steps will ensure goals are responsible and ambitious while also holding the company accountable to consumers and stakeholders to see the initiatives through to completion.
  Even the best-laid sustainability goals will go unmet without a strong culture designed to guarantee leadership and employee alignment. Sustainability must be ingrained in a startup’s culture — from the top down and bottom up — and there’s a lot at stake if it’s not.
 Another Revolution Growth portfolio company, the global fintech-revolutionizing startup  Tala, demonstrates how young companies can imbue their cultures with purpose-driven values. While Tala’s mission is to provide credit to the unbanked, the company believes that the consumer’s best interests should always come first. During  2019’s holiday season, Tala contrasted with businesses fueling consumption by instead urging customers in Kenya to  not take out loans, protecting them from predatory unregulated lenders amid a lack of functioning credit bureaus and loan-stacking databases. This forward-looking approach ultimately safeguarded Tala’s customers and its vibrant digital lending industry.
 Beyond determining what they stand for, many of our portfolio companies face challenges securing talent. People have choices about where they want to work, and those with intrinsic motivations — such as concerns about the environment — will feel uncomfortable if their employers do not share their values. Regulatory risks and customer attrition pale in comparison to the human cost of losing star performers who seek other work cultures that better align with their values.
 A clear values system should embed sustainability into the decision-making process, make obvious imperatives and empower employees to follow through.
  Companies aren’t only judged by their own initiatives — they’re also judged by their partners. As startups build new relationships or expand to work with new suppliers, investors will be keen to know that these outside parties align with their stated sustainability philosophies.
 Before becoming publicly involved with another company, a startup should gauge each new supplier’s reputation, including insights into their employment practices. Take leading Mediterranean fast-casual restaurant  Cava or healthy-inspired salad-centric chain  Sweetgreen, both Revolution Growth portfolio companies; neither will source proteins from farms with inhumane policies. If companies are not aware of these factors, their customers will eventually let them know, and likely hold them accountable for the oversight.
 Think of it this way: If a diagram of your partnerships and supplier relationships was printed on the front page of The New York Times, would you be comfortable with what it shows the world? Today, companies of all sizes are being held to a higher standard by consumers, employees, potential partners and the media. It’s no longer possible to fly under the radar with relationships that are antithetical to a company’s sustainability goals. So take a hard look at your supplier and partner ecosystem, and make clear that you are bringing your green vision to life through every extension of your business.
  Financial realism acknowledges that a company can want to do good, but unless they have the economics, they won’t survive to make an impact. For most startups, beginning with financial realism as a mindset and incrementalism as an approach will be key to success, enabling all businesses to contribute to a more resilient planet. For startups that prioritize environmentally friendly business practices alongside a product or service, this strategy can prevent goodness from becoming the enemy of greatness. Founders in this position can commit to a stage-by-stage sustainability plan, rather than expecting an overnight transformation. Investors understand the delicate balance between striving to meet green goals and keeping the lights on.
 Entrepreneurs looking to build a business that not only adopts eco-friendly practices but also has sustainability at its heart may have to consider starting in a niche industry or market that is less price-sensitive and ready for a solution today. Once that solution is firmly established, the business can build upon what they’ve created, rather than going big with something that doesn’t scale — and failing fast. Without an initial set of customers that value and love what you’re doing, you won’t get to the bigger play.
 As the public and private sectors continue to address the climate crisis, sustainability will increasingly become a mandate rather than an option, and funding will increasingly flow to startups that have addressed potential environmental concerns. Unfortunately, pressure for companies to meet sustainability demands has led to “greenwashing” — the deceptive use of green marketing to persuade consumers that a company’s products, aims and policies are environmentally friendly.
 Greenwashing has forced investors to look beyond mere words for action. As we move toward a more sustainable future, startups pursuing VC funding will need to prove to investors that sustainability is a priority across their entire organizations, aligning their outreach, public commitments and cultures with accountability and concrete examples of sustainable activities. Even if those examples are just steps toward larger goals, they will show investors and customers that startups are ready today to contribute to a greener and better tomorrow.
title: 创始人必须显示投资者，可持续性不仅仅是唇部服务
title_en: Founders must show investors that sustainability is more than lip service

  起初，我得到了积极的回应。但后来，30分钟后，outfoout，replit被指责我不道德的行为，偷了他们的人： 
现在，我在我的开源项目中使用的想法都没有“InternalDesign决策”：他们都在储存的人中公开发布（我知道这一点，因为我被要求在实习期间写一些博客）。我的项目也没有更多的是克隆的任何其他网站，而不是在谷歌的前几页的“运行python在线”，其中大多数看起来完全是笨蛋：
    （这封电子邮件的其余部分基本上我重复了本文的上一个，但有了更多的技术细节。You an阅读Imgur上的整个电子邮件，镜像在incellentArchive上。）
 不幸的是，Replit拒绝向他们说出我做错的人提供任何细节，重申他们以前的陈述，并威胁着我的诉讼：
  然后只是为了把樱桃放在上面，忏悔给我发给我另一个电子邮件，他们只是从投资者的投资者筹集了2000万美元，而且他们不怕将它用反对我。在刚从大学AYEAR之前毕业的实习生中的“我”中的“我”在毕业，谁没有运行任何类型的商业操作。
    关于“像这样的犯罪”的评论 - 这实际上是误导性。在我的项目中只有一个提交，提到沉积，它是我早些时候已经向你展示过的，从我的第三天才添加了所有38种语言恢复支持，展开我想要添加的178种其他语言。
 关于我的评论是一个“苛刻的”实习生 - 我并不是其实际上这意味着意志意味着什么，特别是因为那天早些时候，自追求刚刚招募我（见他们的第一电子邮件的屏幕截图）。但我会独自留下它，因为它对掌握问题并不是非常相关的。
  当然，我立即取下了我的项目，给了它一些时间才能冷却，并派遣了道歉。我认为某些东西已经过电子邮件丢失了，所以我要求打电话： 
不幸的是，Replit忽略了这封电子邮件，所以我将另一个人送去了。这个得到了回应，但不是我希望的那个：
  只是为了案例储存不明白我不太可能与thissituation，我发送了三个后续电子邮件，如今，这一切都忽略了这一点。
 换句话说，Replit通过其威胁站立：如果我重新发布Myopen-Source项目，那么他们会用“Top Lawyers”来起诉我。
     在为他们工作后，开发一个像复合的开源项目是不道德的吗？
 问题1和2有公平的技术细节，所以我已经在一个单独的帖子中。 TL; DR是：
 我的项目并没有比其他15次（商业公司！）在Google上搜索通过搜索“运行Pythononline”或“在线编程环境”来储存的项目。
 我的项目和replit之间的每一个相似性都可以在Github存储库和博客帖子中解释，博客帖子由Reptit本身发布，使其显然没有任何秘密。 
问：在为他们工作后，开发一个垂直项目的开源项目是不道德的吗？
  riju完全是非商业的。与Replit不同，我没有从任何来源广告，捐赠，筹款，订阅，随中征收。我没有兴趣运行业务，从来没有真正想要riju变得过于受欢迎，因为我是一个支付服务器账单的人。
 riju没有窃取顾客的储存。基于我的分析数据，在2月份的riju有38次访问。（那些可能是我的一半。）同时，Replit有超过7milionusers。显然没有意义的RIJU与Replit竞争。
 riju没有建立作为竞争对手的竞争对手。由于TheArchitecture仅限于在单个服务器上运行时，任何人都可以通过在叉车中键入整个系统 - 以及一个我的朋友所做的，只是看看会发生什么。 （系统崩溃了。）IFI正在设计一个与堆积竞争的产品，我当然不会选择只能缩放Totoy-Project大小的架构。
 Replit的核心价值主张不会让您在线运行代码（您可以免费执行此操作），这是运行代码顶部的Fearlesthey优惠。 riju小分类缺少了所有这些功能，包括：拥有用户帐户，保存工作，共享您的工作，分享WebApps，持久性工作空间，讨论论坛，与GitHub集成等等。
 在开发riju时，我没有糟糕的意图，并没有试图隐藏任何东西。作为这些索赔的证据，我提供了我从恐怖宁的Github上公开的项目，以及求出的事实是，因为我公开分享了我自己的意志，Extendingan为他们提供灵感我的工作。
 riju从来没有打算成为一个产品，它旨在成为Aperonal Playground / Art件。作为这一索赔的证据，我提供了我花了几个小时的时间添加了像六角形和Snobol这样的语言，而是为了让你的工作（！）。 
我不是一个商务人士。我只是一个喜欢兴趣的开源开发人士才能获得乐趣。 （如果您怀疑没有赚钱的建筑物的曲目记录，只需查看MyWebite上的列表，并记录了占收入的一分钱的显着缺席。）
 在用纯藏后，我永远不会试图偷走某人的生意。伤害沉积不是我在riju工作的意图，并否则致追我 - 特别是在没有要求单一的澄清时，并拒绝所有优惠有讨论 - 在我看来，在附上的储存中显示出的糟糕信仰。
 如果你想自己决定谁是右边的，我很高兴地展开所有的证据（除了代码除外，如果我这样做，因为我的承诺为我的承诺）。同样，我有一个有了所有技术细节的一篇章。您可以在IMGUR上阅读我和Replit之间的所有通信，在IMGUR上，镜像在InteRAsharive上）。（关于Reptit Internals编辑的技术细节，以及可以间接透露这些细节的Anystatement。）
   鼓励开源开发（例如，Of.Their技术开放的部分，添加GitHub SpectsToreplit; Replit的CEO：“我欠我的整个职业创作源”）
 回馈社区（从博客邮政追求问我送货上线：“我们正在迁移[...]，以改善各地的开发人员的生态系统”;“回馈社区。我们的语言无社会包管理的核心现在开源Ongithub“）
 使您易于分享和混合您的创作（例如，replit的Ceoon学习编程：“我会下载与我想要构建的内容相关的项目，与他们一起修改，而GetInepired”;他向换句们提供建议Fork和Remixtoo，并将这种哲学销售为offlit的差异化特征）
  他们说他们鼓励开源开发，但是当Myopen-Source Project冒犯了他们时，他们用extremprejudice关闭它 
他们声称通过他们的Openen-Source和博客帖子送回社区，但是当我试图在非友好项目中使用这些想法时，他们威胁要起诉我
 他们说他们让他们很容易分享和混合你的创作 - 但是我试图重新修复自己，我成为一个人的非格拉塔
 在对我的回复的电子邮件中，他们的威胁是基于过去为他们在过去为他们工作的事实。但是，我相信这个推理的ISA烟幕。要了解为什么，让我们来看看一些TheReplit的首席执行官，Amjad，发布在通过电子邮件威胁我之后发布：
     在这些推文中，Amjad指出，雅典的研究被另一家公司显然被淘汰，因此他们应该是“嘲笑”并禁止接受资金。此外，他说：“我站在我所说的副本总体上”，即，他蔑视他认为他认为“复制”现有的人，而不仅仅是他的推文中的公司。
 Amjad对Athensresearch的评论和他对自己项目的法律威胁之间有明显的相似之处。他甚至在两者中使用了相同的词，“CopyCat”。在雅典学习的情况下，没有什么可以做的，以表达他的诋毁他们在推特上诋毁他们。但在我的情况下，自从我之前工作过，Amjad有一个借口抛弃了我的指责，如果你不熟悉事实，那么他们可能是合法的声音。
 我还要重申，随着“最高律师”和“大量资金”的人威胁，是一个没有公司的新毕业生，没有资金，没有商业野心。如果具有实际商业企业的人是冒犯追求，我会颤抖着思考他们可能会收到的那样。
   早上发布了这篇文章后，它在哈克内德的讨论之后。 5点下午5点左右，在黑客新闻中发表了道歉，这也是众所周知的。 
在通话期间，Amjad为做出法律威胁而道歉，勇敢的是他觉得我曾经催富过克里特，并且我不道德地发作了。最终，我们同意不同意，他赞美他的写作，他对我的项目备份时很好：
   没有道歉（或提及）公开讨厌他的决定威胁我的决定，并且在电话呼叫之后没有发布到TheThread的更新
 对我“字面上偷走和出版的IP”的公开发布者指控并不道歉，尽管HEDID至少删除了转关
 没有道歉（或提到）在雇用我之后，我是一件模糊的个人攻击，我是一个“苛刻的实习生”
 在电话里说，在“道歉”之后，他感到asif他邀请我进入他的房子，我背叛了他的信任
 仍然拒绝列出他认为我的任何特定部分，他认为我在电话中向他询问了这些细节时，尽管他继续私下和公开，但我是不道德的
 以书面形式误导了我，让我看起来像我同意他的致命，就在向他的行为道歉之后道歉 
尽管如此，我很高兴我们能够充分地实现这种情况，我期待着与我的生命的riju恢复继续前进，我可以再次编译东西:)
note_en:
Ithink you should take it down and stop working on it. I'll be engagingour lawyers on Monday if it's still up by then.  [...] We were a tiny company when you internedwith us  [...] Luckily we're bigger now,and crucially have a lot of money to pay for top lawyers now if we'reforced to go that route.
 I received an official response from Replit and my open-sourceproject will be back up soon; please see  the bottom of the blogpost for an update. Therest of the content here will remain as a historical artifact.
 Hi, my name is Radon. I graduated college last year and now work as asoftware engineer in DevOps/Infrastructure. In my free time, I alsomaintain  a number of open-sourceprojects.
 While I was in college, I interned at the startup Replit. This blogpost is the story of how Replit is using legal threats and theirventure-capital funding to bully me into shutting down an open-sourceproject they don’t like.
    Replit makes a webapp you can use to run code online in differentprogramming languages. This is nothing new ( just Google “run pythononline” forproof), so Replit’s value proposition is extra features like sharingyour work, installing third-party packages, and hosting webapps.
 I worked for Replit in Summer 2019, where I was asked to rebuildReplit’s package management stack and make it open-source. If you likereading about tech stuff,  here’s the post I wrote for Replit’sblog,and  here’s the code on GitHub.
 I took a job elsewhere in Summer 2020, but still chatted with themoccasionally by email when they reached out to tell me about somethingcool Replit had developed.
    The aspect of Replit that I really enjoyed was how it supported lotsof different programming languages. (I wrote  another blog post forReplitabout how they do that.) That got me thinking: how many programminglanguages could you possibly cram into a single website?
 To explore that question, I put together my own little webapp thatcould run code online. After about a day, I had something that worked.(If you’re wondering why it was so fast—it turns out you only need 30 lines of code to letpeople run Python code in a webapp! This may be why there are so manywebsites for running Python online…)
 After it was working, I started adding as many different programminglanguages as I could. As you can see from this excerpt of my project’sversion history, I got a little overexcited:
 languages ---------2020-06-05 df9ba38 Initial commit 02020-06-05 5e3a4a4 Install some packages into a Docker image 02020-06-05 e937c8f Simple Express server with "Hello world" 02020-06-06 0961498 Embed terminal on frontend app 02020-06-06 c66cf63 Embed Monaco editor on frontend 02020-06-06 27ab1f7 Add "run" button 02020-06-06 f417858 You can run Python code now 12020-06-07 d543081 You can run many languages now 82020-06-07 e2a3e71 All languages 17 working now 172020-06-07 473c50c ALL THE LANGUAGES 252020-06-08 3718315 even more languages 332020-06-08 548c1c1 repl.it superiority!! 382020-06-08 1ae424f More languages, we need all the languages 482020-06-09 c34ccf2 A lot more languages 772020-06-09 846caf2 At this point the number of languages is absurd 79
 I eventually ended up with 216 languages, including  all 38 languagesfromReplit, all 100 languages from Yusuke Endoh’s “QuineRelay”, and a good deal morebesides. You might ask: Why did I spend so much time adding obscureprogramming languages to a webapp nobody was going to use? Well, letme put it this way: Is it the weirdest 2020 hobby you’ve seen?
  One day, I got an email from Replit letting me know about a newfeature they released. I figured this was a good time to share myopen-source project with them, in case they wanted to take inspirationfrom any of my work:
  At first, I got a positive response. But then, 30 minutes later, outof nowhere, Replit accused me of unethical behavior and stealing theirdesign:
   Now, none of the ideas I used in my open-source project were “internaldesign decisions”: they’ve all been published publicly on Replit’sblog (I knew this because I’d been asked to  write some of those blogposts during my internship). And my project also wasn’t any more of aReplit clone than any of the other websites on the first few pages ofGoogle results for “run python online”, most of which look exactly thesame:
    (The rest of this email is basically me repeating the previousparagraph of this article, but with a lot more technical details. Youcan read the whole email  on Imgur,mirrored  on the InternetArchive.)
 Unfortunately, Replit refused to provide any specifics on what theywere saying I had done wrong, reiterated their previous statements,and threatened me with a lawsuit:
  And then just to put a cherry on top, Replit sent me another emailreminding me that they  just raised $20 million from their investorslastmonth,and they weren’t afraid to use it against me. The “me” in questionbeing one of their previous interns who just graduated from college ayear ago, and who isn’t running any kind of commercial operationwhatsoever.
    The remark about “commits like this”—this is actually misleading.There’s only one commit in my project that mentions Replit, and it’sthe one I already showed you earlier, from my third day of coding,when I’d just added all 38 languages that Replit supported, beforemoving onto the 178 other languages I wanted to add.
 The remark about me being a “demanding” intern—I’m not actuallysure what this is meant to imply, especially since Replit had justtried to recruit me earlier that day (see the screenshot of theirfirst email). But I’ll leave it alone because it’s not reallyrelevant to the issue at hand.
  Naturally, I took down my project right away, gave it some time forfeelings to cool, and sent Replit an apology. I figured somethingmight have been lost over email, so I asked to get on a call:
  Unfortunately, Replit ignored this email, so I sent them another onefollowing up. This one got a response, but not the one I was hopingfor:
  Just in case Replit didn’t understand that I wasn’t OK with thissituation, I sent three follow-up emails explaining as such over thenext few weeks, all of which were ignored.
 In other words, Replit stands by its threat: if I re-publish myopen-source project, then they will sue me with “top lawyers”.
     Was it unethical for me to develop an open-source project that’ssimilar to Replit, after working for them?
 Questions 1 and 2 have a fair number of technical details, so I’ve putthem  in a separate post. The TL;DR is:
 My project isn’t any more similar to Replit than the 15 other(commercial!) ones you can find on Google by searching “run pythononline” or “online programming environment”.
 Every similarity between my project and Replit can be explained bylooking only at GitHub repositories and blog posts that were published online by Replit itself, making them obviously not anykind of secret.
  Q: Was it unethical for me to develop an open-source project that’ssimilar to Replit, after working for them?
  Riju is entirely non-commercial. Unlike Replit, I didn’t seekfunding from any source—advertising, donations, fundraising,subscriptions, whatever. I have no interest in running a business,and never really  wanted Riju to become too popular, since I wasthe one paying the server bill.
 Riju wasn’t stealing customers from Replit. Based on my analyticsdata, there were 38 visits to Riju during the month of February.(Half of those were probably me.) Meanwhile, Replit had  over 7millionusers.There’s obviously no sense in which Riju was competing with Replit.
 Riju wasn’t  built as a competitor to Replit, either. Since thearchitecture was limited to running on a single server, anyone couldbring the entire system down just by typing in a  forkbomb—and one of myfriends did, just to see what would happen. (The system crashed.) IfI were designing a product to compete with Replit, I certainlywouldn’t have picked an architecture that could only scale totoy-project size.
 Replit’s core value proposition isn’t letting you run code online(you can do this in dozens of places for free), it’s the featuresthey offer on top of running code. Riju categorically lacked all ofthese features, including: having a user account, saving your work,sharing your work, publishing webapps, persistent workspaces,discussion forums, integration with GitHub, etc. etc.
 I had no bad intentions towards Replit when developing Riju, andwasn’t trying to hide anything. As proof of these claims, I offerthe fact that I had the project public on my GitHub from thebeginning, and the fact that Replit found out about the projectbecause I openly shared it with them of my own volition, extendingan offer for them to take inspiration from my work.
 Riju was never intended to be a product, it was intended to be apersonal playground / art piece. As proof of this claim, I offer thefact that I spent dozens of hours adding languages like Hexagony and SNOBOL rather making it so you couldsave your work(!).
 I’m not a business person. I’m just an open-source dev who likes tobuild weird things for fun. (If you doubt my track record of buildingthings that don’t make money, just check out  the list on mywebsite, and note the conspicuous absence ofanything that’s ever made a cent of revenue.)
 I would never try to steal someone’s business after I worked withthem. Hurting Replit was not my intent in working on Riju, and toaccuse me otherwise—especially without asking a single clarifyingquestion, and refusing all offers to have a discussion—shows a greatdeal of bad faith on the part of Replit, in my opinion.
 If you’d like to decide for yourself who is in the right, I’m happy toput all of the evidence out in the open (except the code, becauseReplit’s standing by its promise to sue me if I do). Again, I have  aseparate post with all the technical details. And you canalso read all communications between me and Replit, in full andunabridged form,  on Imgur, mirrored  onthe InternetArchive).(Technical details about Replit internals redacted, as well as anystatement that could reveal such details indirectly.)
   encouraging open-source development (e.g.  open-sourcing parts oftheir technology,  adding GitHub supporttoReplit;Replit’s CEO:  “I owe my entire career toopen-source”)
 giving back to the community (from  the blog post Replit asked me towrite:“we are migrating […] to do our part in improving the ecosystemfor developers everywhere”; “Giving back to the community. The coreof our language-agnostic package management is now open-source onGitHub”)
 making it easy to share and remix your creations (e.g. Replit’s CEOon learning to program:  “I’d download projects related to what Iwanted to build […] change & tinker with them, and getinspired”;he advises others to forkand remixtoo, and sells this philosophy as  a differentiating feature ofReplit)
  they say they encourage open-source development, but when myopen-source project offended them, they shut it down with extremeprejudice
 they claim to be giving back to the community through theiropen-source and blog posts, but when I tried to use those ideas in acommunity project, they threatened to sue me
 they say they make it easy to share and remix your creations—butwhen I tried to remix Replit itself, I became a  persona non grata
 In Replit’s emails to me, their threats were based on the fact that Ihad worked for them in the past. However, I believe this reasoning isa smokescreen. To see why, let’s take a look at some tweets thatReplit’s CEO, Amjad, posted just after threatening me by email:
     In these tweets, Amjad points out that Athens Research was clearlyinspired by another company, and argues that they should therefore be“ridiculed” and banned from receiving funding. Furthermore, he says:“I stand by what I said about copycats in general”, i.e. he disdainsall people that he thinks are “copying” existing ones, not just thespecific company in his tweet.
 There is a clear resemblance between Amjad’s comments on AthensResearch and his legal threats towards my own project. He even usedexactly the same word, “copycat”, in both. In the case of AthensResearch, there was nothing Amjad could do to express his disdainbesides denigrate them on Twitter. But in my case, since I had workedfor Replit before, Amjad had an excuse to throw accusations myway—accusations that, if you aren’t familiar with the facts, soundlike they could be legitimate.
 I’d also like to reiterate that the person Replit is threatening with“top lawyers” and “a lot of money” is a  new grad with  no company, no funding, and  no commercial ambitions. If someone with an actualcommercial enterprise were to offend Replit, I shudder to think whattreatment  they might receive.
   After I posted the article in the morning, it was  discussedextensively on HackerNews. Around 5pm,Amjad posted an apology on Hacker News,  which has also beendiscussed.
    During the call, Amjad apologized for making legal threats, butreiterated that he felt I had made a clone of Replit and that I hadacted unethically. Eventually we agreed to disagree, and he promisedin writing that he was fine with my putting my project back up:
   didn’t apologize for (or mention)  publicly doublingdownon his decision to threaten me, and didn’t post an update to thatthread after the phone call
 didn’t apologize for (or mention)  publiclyretweetingan accusation that I “literally stole and published IP”, although hedid at least delete the retweet
 didn’t apologize for (or mention) making a  nebulous personalattack in writing that I was a “demanding intern”right after  trying to hire me
 said in the phone call, right after “apologizing”, that he felt asif he had invited me into his house and I had betrayed his trust
 still refused to list any specific part of Replit he thought I hadcopied, even when I asked him for such details multiple times duringthe phone call, despite his continuing to claim both privately andpublicly that I copied Replit unethically
 misquoted me in writing to make me look like I agreed with hisaccusations, right after reaching out to apologize for his behavior
  Nonetheless, I’m happy that we were able to get the situation workedout amiably, and I look forward to moving on with my life—includingputting Riju back up, after I can get things compiling again :)
title: replit如何回复此博客文章？
title_en: How did Replit respond to this blog post?

note:
该消息通过Apple的网站上的支持文档，由MacRumors发现。
 为什么？简而言之：支持它并没有真正有意义，它不再支持它 -  iTunes不再是全部包含的克拉姆 -  It-in-In-In-In-In-In-In-In empst，它曾经是的，所以今天这个名字并没有真正有意义。 Apple指出，他们已经建立了课堂和课堂等独立应用程序，以帮助教师分发材料和等级学生;与此同时，大量第三方应用程序现在关注数字教育作为首次和最重要的特征。
  Apple于2007年首次推出iTunes U作为iTunes Store的一部分，意图举办“Top Us College”的课程和内容。到2013年，Apple表示，这些课程已下载超过十亿次。然而，到2019年中旬，它正在将大部分内容转换为其他平台（例如将opon-occess内容移动到Apple Podcasts应用程序），似乎是准备最终关机。
 Apple表示，iTunes上的所有目前的内容都将保持“通过2020  -  2012年的教育年度”，但鼓励教师比以后更快地备份材料。如果您是一名学生在iTunes课程上使用课程，同时，您需要与教师联系，以弄清楚他们打算移动它的情况。
note_en:
The news comes via  a support document on Apple’s site, as spotted  by MacRumors.
 Why? In short: It doesn’t really make sense to support it anymore — iTunes is no longer the all-encompassing, cram-it-on-in feature beast that it once was, so the name doesn’t really make sense today. Apple points out that they’ve been building out standalone apps like Classroom and Schoolwork to help teachers distribute material and grade students; meanwhile, plenty of third-party apps now focus on digital education as their first and foremost feature.
  Apple first launched iTunes U in 2007 as a section of the iTunes Store meant to host courses and content from “top US colleges.” By 2013, Apple said those courses had been downloaded more than  one billion times. By the middle of 2019, however, it was shifting much of the content to other platforms (such as moving audio-only content to the Apple Podcasts app), seemingly in preparation for an eventual shutdown.
 Apple says that all current content on iTunes U will stay up “through the 2020-2021 educational year,” but encourages instructors to back up their materials sooner than later. If you’re a student working through a course on iTunes U, meanwhile, you’ll need to reach out to the instructor to figure out if/where they plan on moving it.
title: 苹果在2021年之后停止iTunes U
title_en: Apple to discontinue iTunes U after 2021

注意：这篇文章最初在2016年出现在媒体上。这是一个非常轻微的修订版，具有新格式，以便于可读性。它在历史修正主义和家庭人类学的帖子之前，但它会解决一些相同的主题。
   历史往往被指控不够科学。为了解决这个人试图提出各种历史理论，试图看起来像科学。他们提出了一些可测量的变量（例如，作为贾尔德钻石的可用物种的可用性，因为伊恩莫里斯（Ian Morris）的人口或人口大小，技术进步，军队的规模等等。他们希望，解释过去并预测未来。
 基于理论，愿意科学家 - 历史学家建立模型，这些模型似乎是完美的感觉，但似乎被事件所超越的时候分崩离析。这使得历史看起来并不是所有科学。但这只是因为我们可以将它与罕见的科学中的非常罕见的实例进行比较，在那里可以长期完美预测。喜欢行星体的动作或计算机的行为。
 但在大多数情况下，科学挣扎着完美的预测。在医学中，不可能预测疾病将采取任何一个人的确切课程，即使通常可以预测它需要千个人的课程。在生物学中，无法预测，通过自然选择将在其他方面选择哪些功能。但是可以预测一些人会。但是，对我来说，最好的比喻是天气。
 天气是大量（分接无限）的结果，完全知识，可预测和可预测的物理事件。但是，甚至在几小时或几天内，我们不能完全预测它，而不是几周或几个月。历史（或者在过去或现在或现在或在未来的任何情况下）也是一个相对知名度的大（分形无限）的结果，相对知识，相对描述的和相对可预测的社交事件。然而，我们无法在值得预测的任何规模上预测它。
 我们不会将历史视为科学的原因，但我们认为气象是科学，即使基于观察，已知的规律和常量的构建模型，是因为历史学家可以制作的那种可靠的预测，对任何人都没有用，虽然任何类型的甚至适度准确的天气预报对每个人都非常有用。
 以下是一些关于天气可用于查看历史的事情。在大多数日子里，天气与前一天相同。您可以根据当前的气候预测天气以及您提前几小时内了解世界的天气（即使您仍然犯错误）。预先预测确切的天气（或历史）几个月是不可能预测的，但您可以预测一系列可能的天气模式（例如，欧洲的夏天可以是温暖的或冷酷的，但不会有雪;欧盟的政治关系是温暖的或寒冷但不会有战争）。 
您还可以预测气候变化的趋势，但不是这些模式的确切模式或后果或后果（例如，全球温度会上升，但它意味着它将在法国的夏天定期雪？;欧盟各州之间的分歧将继续但它意味着回归到20世纪50年代的经常性战争吗？）。在造型气候/天气和历史中建模的最常见错误来自于全球的当地（具有气候天气和趋势的趋势） - 例如，在一个寒冷的夏天，人们可能会质疑全球变暖或罗马帝国的堕落在整个历史上都在预测到它，直到它发生了数百年。天气和历史可以被怪胎（黑色天鹅）活动中断。
 这意味着历史可以被认为是比通常声称的更重要的科学，而不必建造看起来像气候学的模型。
  在半技术术语中，使用复杂性理论建模天气。复杂系统的一个特性是对初始条件的敏感性。这意味着初始条件的少量变化可能导致系统状态的大幅波动。这通常被描述为“蝴蝶效应”，这是完全误导地被描述为“蝴蝶拍打其翅膀将导致飓风”的“蝴蝶效果”。 [看看取比太远的危险]
 一个更好的例子可能是在树木繁茂的区域移动的天气前线导致温度的微小变化，然后如果它是+0.5摄氏度，如果它是+1摄氏度，则可以导致飓风发展。既不是州的飓风 - 这是由飓风等大事引起的。但它是结构可能性的初始配置的一部分。
 我们有一组背景，其中某些模式倾向于发生，并且根本不会发生其他模式。天气可能是气候（当地或全球），因此它在北极1月份的热带或下雨不会雪。在历史中，人们无法使用尚未发明的技术，或者通常新的代理商不会出现在蓝色之外。 [这是显而易见的部分。]
 很少，发生怪异的事件。在北极，飓风中可能会在季节，在热带地区的雪花中获得不合时宜的天气。在历史中，蒙古，征服者，伊斯尼斯似乎似乎无处不在（并且没有根据标准假设的模型预测的任何方式）。
 但是在怪物事件之外，您可以在给定的时间段内预测给定位置中的天气模式范围。地球上有一系列天气模式，天气在这些模式设定的限制范围内。你可以为历史做同样的事情。某些类型的事情可能会发生（类似于发生的事情），其他类型不是 - 例如 - 例如外星人入侵，Raptures不太可能发生，而是发生侵犯，内战，饥荒和流行病。 
基于极端短期（小时至天数）的模型，可以预测具有体面精度的本地天气模式，但即使这些预测也可能由于模型中的假设而受到显着的误差。例如。它可能很重要，前面是否越过树木繁茂的地区或一个领域。可以在极端短期 - 小时到几天准确地预测历史事件 - 仅基于各种因果机型（选举结果，预定的事件，推进军队），但由于模型中的假设，它仍然频繁错误
  上面，我使用了“分形无穷大”一词。这不是（据我所知）技术术语。这是一个基于Madelbrot纸的海岸线悖论的隐喻'英国的海岸多长时间'。这是维基百科的描述：
 海岸线的长度取决于用于测量它的方法。由于陆地拥有各种尺度的特点，从数百公里的大小到毫米和下方的小分数，因此应围绕应测量的最小特征的明显尺寸，因此没有单一限定的陆地限定的周边。
 这意味着可能，可能存在无限（或无限制的）数量的测量单元，其是悖论位。但更有趣的是这些海岸线具有跨越尺度相似的模式。这就是Madelbrot所说的方式：
 地理曲线如此涉及他们的细节，即它们的长度通常是无限的或相当难以理解的。然而，许多人在统计上“自我灵活”，意思是每个部分可以被认为是整体的减小的图像。
 为什么这与历史相关？我不想过度比喻。 Mandelbrot的贡献是这些类型的几何对象的数学描述，并且在某些可量化的环境中运作良好。但这不是历史需求等社交对象的研究。那些相同的量化不会在更多的模拟问题上工作。然而，这些类比可以解释历史分析中的一个巨大问题 - 即在巨大不同的放大水平上的类似模式的混合。
 想象历史（即，随着时间的推移，事件显示为人类观察者）作为风景。当你从一定的高度飞过景观时，不同的区域似乎非常相似或可能相同（从足够大的距离，地球看起来像一个点）。所以，从足够的距离似乎似乎争取争夺人群资源。因此，它将有意义地描述历史。 
当您更近时，您将在资源的斗争中看到很大的不等式，因此您可能希望在项目电力的能力方面描述历史。您可能希望量化这些差异。
 但是，如果你缩小更近，你会看到看起来非常相似的模式。尽管这些团体具有不同的尺寸，但它们都有非常类似的层次组织 - 在顶部有某种领导，必须随时间变化。因此，您将尝试提出这些层次结构的形成规则。
 然而，当你缩小更多并且你会注意到完全不同的海关，谈判方式，合法化的方式。因此，您将假定完整的缩进性，简单地描述差异（种类的类别是分类师生物学家）。
 但是，你在个人动机的水平上放大了更多，你会看到欲望，饥饿，目标，争取个人成功，家庭 - 然后你可以假设我们都是一样的。
 但问题表明，在彼此方面描述不同的放大率，因为它们是彼此的一种类似的模型。在争取资源方面，个人的欲望和梦想可以重新循环;可以在个人欲望方面谈论王国水平的行为和习俗。
 因此，历史的“维度”取决于用于测量它的尺度的长度。您可以从“历史大人物”的角度来形容同样的事件，“社会力量”的观点，为资源的角度斗争或“长杜勒”，历史视角不可避免的力量。就像你可以在结构水平，分子相互作用或物质转化的水平描述化学事件。
 但所有这些层次/观点都有自己的规则和看似因果模式。他们也互相互动，但以复杂的方式互动，这妨碍了简单的减速。也就是说，我们不能说，在一个放大率的相互作用，以与单个砖（或铺设）相同的方式相同，使得不导致墙壁存在。 
我来了像“墙上隐喻”的因果关系。我们对导致事件的因果链有完全不同的直觉，导致墙壁的存在，而不是我们对历史事件产生的事件链。
 但也许在墙壁上重新思考历史事件可能会有所帮助 - 只要我们也保持思想的差异。我们有砖制造商，砖层，还有专员，批准，天气条件，基础，砖制作的历史习俗和砖铺设 - 所有这些都在我们要得到的墙上发挥作用。
 很明显，根据我们的观点，因果链将完全不同。当我们试图摧毁墙壁时也是如此。我们需要相称的力量 - 足够大的力量来制作一个凹痕，但我们也需要很多小事到分子水平。而不同的观点将识别不同的因果链。所有这些都是正确的，但显然属于某些水平。当墙壁来说，这些都很明显（或相当明显）。但在历史上不太明显。
 大多数好的历史学家似乎似乎拥有与我们大多数人对墙壁有关的事件的同样的直觉。但他们经常被历史类型的习俗诱惑，写入狂热的他们的直觉，并以减少的观点来。
  我们倾向于在历史上发生因果关系，正如在最低放大率水平和累积到总数的情况下发生的因果关系。在最简单的账户中，这是完美的意义。很多苹果放入篮筐+篮子里，将制作一个充满苹果的篮子。但这是一种无益的方式来看待因果关系，因为它意味着一定程度的原子学 - 进一步不可分割的测量单位的最终水平。但那个级别不能存在 - 或者如果它确实存在它必须如此低（解封），整个不能使用它建模。即使我们相信某种终端粒子，它也是一个傻瓜的差事（看着你的斯蒂芬霍金）试图实际使用它来测量对象级现象。
 这就是为什么导致飓风的蝴蝶是对初始条件敏感性的差异的差。没有物理世界的一个方面'导致天气 - 不是额外的+ 5摄氏度的温度，也不是月亮或海湾流的引力。他们在各级系统中不断互动。但是我们对天气的模型能力依赖于测量中的微小偏差。
 和历史和其他社会制度也是如此。除了在最琐碎的意义上，没有一个事件导致另一个事件。累积小事的积累不会“原因”大事。因为没有最小的事件，可以说是构成初始条件的最终放大水平。但是我们对小事的能力确实对我们建模未来事件模式的能力产生了巨大影响。 
拿破仑在胜利期间赢得它的战斗期间左右，拿破仑看起来可能会产生一些差异，这反过来导致他对普鲁士州的膨胀。这反过来可能对第一次世界大战或第三帝国的形状产生了差异。但这一瞥留下了实际造成这些东西或导致这些东西吗？不是在时间旅行冒险小说的观点。它只是在一定水平的放大级别的不同状态下的模式（初始条件）只是一个少量差异。
 但是在另一个层面上，即使拿破仑从未出生过，那么系统可能看起来非常相同，从一定程度的放大率（欧洲国家为资源挣扎）。
 我们可以拥有大事导致其他大事以及小事造成其他小事的模型。我们必须小心始终知道我们正在谈论的倍数。但我们必须记住我们正在谈论我们的模型，而不是实际发生的事情。模型更大，初始条件的测量中的较大误差 - 或者与大型模型相反，我们实际上没有希望完全测量初始条件或任何类型的计算途径，即使这种测量也是可能的。
 因此，即使是非常好的和完整的历史模型，定义也没有办法预测任何级别的准确性融入任何类型的未来。并且我们无法在过去的事件上使用他们的准确性，因为我们对过去的数据的测量已经过滤通过发生的事情（即历史记录是由获奖者编写的 - 或者记录守门员）。我们没有那种过滤器的目前或最近的历史。
 我们可以使用过去的信息来帮助我们通过现在的数据筛选，但这就是初始条件的谎言，可以完全搞砸我们的预测。即使我们有一个单独的心理和小型事件动态的合法模型 - 以同样的方式，天气建模者具有准确的分子和牛顿对象物理学的模型 - 我们仍然无法在预测上做得更好，而不是天气人们可以做得更好。事实上，在预测中的任何个人成功都无法在长期运行中保证模型的质量。
  这篇论文是由最近对Jared Diamond现在经典但高度争议的“枪支，细菌和钢铁”的播客讨论的启发。但我在阅读Ian Morris的'为什么西方规则'和Niall Ferguson的“文明”时，我真的开始思考它。他们都遭受了无意识的倍率重新焦点的问题。
 弗格森 - 迄今为止谁是三个中最卑鄙的思想家，虽然绝不是一个毫无价值的人 - 是最重要的例子。他甚至无法修复西方的想法超过一篇一章。 
另一方面，莫里斯在他的假设和测量中是最严格的 - 实际上创造了两本书 - 这个论点和脚注。但即使他似乎压缩了时间段，并在效果尺寸和尺度上播放，以使整个事物工​​作。史蒂文粉红色同样的事情在令人畏惧的缺陷中，但值得阅读“我们性质的更好的天使”。
 我认为这些是现代史学末世论，重要的是阅读良好的人类学家，以充分了解这些人正在读出的东西。
 所有这些都是所有这些都是大卫格拉伯伯的“债务：最近对我而言最近（虽然在出版日期最早）Eric Wolf的'欧洲和没有历史的人民'。也值得阅读是大西洋历史上最近的书籍。但不可忽视的兴趣是甚至渣滓就像Pat Buchanan的“西方的衰落”，因为它为一个例子思考了原因的例子。
 他们都认为历史理论作为关于历史因果关系的一个假设的集合。但我们已经知道了因果链是什么。或者，好历史学家做。但是，当在良好的历史理论上，他们忘记了大多数人。从天气跳到气候学，但随后使用气候的语言来谈谈今天对我们的课程的天气或当他们放大历史时期的时间很好。
 捍卫钻石时CGP灰色建议人们继续批评所有小事的钻石，但他们从未显着批评他的宏伟叙事。这也是我对许多批评的初始印象。
 但对钻石的批评实际上是关于他从他的资源利用水平的高度俯冲建模历史的高度，以便他在令人震惊的个人事件的水平上，试图在小事上使用相同的模型。因此，这导致了美洲发生的事情的完全不准确的描述 - 枪支和钢铁似乎几乎没有似乎并不相对较少（如此以上）。
 在“新”世界的征服是当地政治的征服 - 征服者在当地政治中陷入困境，他们的胜利是与其他当地派系的联盟的结果。与此同时，葡萄牙人没有什么可以发生在非洲西海岸或印度的任何事情（即使他们赢得了对奥斯曼斯的一些重要的海军胜利，也可以像钢铁，更好的枪支和同样的细菌一样）。 
在某种程度上，钻石的批评者（谁不会在Nra Rally被抓住）都说'枪不会杀人，人们杀了人。 他们在说，真正的人杀死并奴役了其他真人 - 我们必须判断所发生的事情发生了什么。 这不是枪支，细菌或钢铁。 这是我们所做的祖先。 我们还在做它 - 尽管是一种不那么无意义的种族灭绝的方式。  Diamond的说法是消除对当前动力装置差异的种族优越性解释。 他解释了一部分。 但他不在乎  ......
